insight.headline: Storefront API update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive can be used to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel.
insight.developer_impact: Developers using the Storefront API should update their queries to include the channelId argument when necessary. This change enables more flexibility in querying data specific to different sales channels. Developers should review their existing queries and update them to take advantage of this new feature. The GraphQL Admin API and other APIs remain unaffected by this change.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants will benefit from more accurate and sales channel-specific data in their storefronts, potentially leading to improved customer experiences.
insight.why_it_matters: This update provides more flexibility and control over data querying, enabling developers to build more tailored and sales channel-specific storefront experiences. It is an important update for developers using the Storefront API, especially those working with multiple sales channels.

also_worth_knowing.text: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate

updates.title: Physical inventory feature preview
updates.summary: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, allowing early access to new inventory APIs. Enable it on a development store to build and test against it.
CLI version 4.5.2 fixes a warning when running theme commands in valid theme directories and bumps Shopify/theme-tools packages. This update improves the overall stability of the CLI.

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, affecting apps that store cardholder data. This change enhances security but may require updates to affected apps.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2 adds non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and allows bypassing confirmation prompts in hydrogen upgrade, enhancing the development experience.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: Updated App Store requirements include using a unique name for apps to reduce merchant confusion, affecting app developers and merchants alike.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: The Storefront API @inContext directive now supports the channelId argument, allowing for more specific sales channel context in queries.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: Fields productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle have been added to ExchangeLineItem, providing more detailed information.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 includes discount allocations to bundle components, allowing apps to access component-level discount allocations.
CLI version 4.5.1 updates cli-hydrogen to 13.0.1, addressing issues and improving stability.
CLI version 4.5.0 introduces new store commands, including listing and store creation, enhancing the development experience.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1
updates.summary: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1 reads disabled command metadata and stops those commands with an actionable error, preserving build errors during deployment.

updates.title: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38
updates.summary: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38 adds an explicit app alias to new Hydrogen projects and improves JavaScript project configuration.

recap.headline: Shopify updates GraphQL Admin API, Storefront API, and CLI with new features and requirements

themes.title: GraphQL Admin API updates
themes.detail: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, and the card-deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate. Additionally, new fields have been added to the ExchangeLineItem type.

themes.title: Storefront API and Hydrogen updates
themes.detail: The Storefront API now supports the channelId argument in the @inContext directive, and Hydrogen has been updated to version 13.0.2 with new flags for non-interactive shop and storefront creation.

themes.title: CLI updates and App Store requirements
themes.detail: The Shopify CLI has been updated to version 4.5.2 with new store commands and bug fixes, and the App Store requirements have been updated to include a unique name for each app.

actions.text: Enable physical inventory feature preview on a development store
Update apps to use Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate for card-deposit endpoint
Review and update app names to comply with new App Store requirements
